title: ranunculus tricuspis var. variifolia
intro: Ranunculus tricuspis var. variifolia, also known as the 'Variable Three-angled Buttercup,' is a plant species belonging to the Ranunculus genus, characterized by its delicate white flowers and three-angled leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus tricuspis var. variifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically taken orally or applied topically as a poultice or salve.
